step_by_step_thinking:
Ossifying fibromas typically present with well-circumscribed, unilocular radiopacities on imaging studies. This is due to their characteristic calcified matrix which gives them density similar to bone.

My Answer: B
Your reasoning accurately reflects the typical appearance of an ossifying fibroma on imaging. These lesions often appear as single (unilocular), dense areas that have higher attenuation than surrounding soft tissues or cancellous bone due to abundant collagen and calcium deposits within the tumor.
